core_rule: A monolithic problem is unactionable; an atom is claimable. Decompose first; work second.
granularity_rule: Anything beyond 4h estimated effort MUST be re-decomposed. 4h is the upper limit.

claim_protocol.rule_1: Single-writer wins. Atomic update of status/claimed_by/claimed_at to BOTH ATOMS.md and ATOMS.json in one unit of work.
claim_protocol.rule_2: JSON mirror is canonical for race resolution. When MD and JSON disagree on status, JSON wins.
claim_protocol.stale_claim_rule: claimed > 24h with no in_progress_at — any subsequent agent may release back to available.

required_fields_schema.title: string (imperative)
required_fields_schema.status: available | claimed | in_progress | completed | blocked | abandoned

lifecycle_promotions.available_to_claimed: Agent atomically writes status/claimed_by/claimed_at; first write wins.
lifecycle_promotions.claimed_to_in_progress: Agent sets in_progress_at when work begins.
lifecycle_promotions.in_progress_to_completed: Agent verifies acceptance criteria; sets completed_at/completed_by/verification.
lifecycle_promotions.in_progress_to_blocked: Agent surfaces blocking dependency in blocked_by or notes.
lifecycle_promotions.in_progress_to_abandoned: Agent decides this isn't right work; appends notes explaining.
lifecycle_promotions.blocked_to_claimed: Block clears; original claimant may resume.
lifecycle_promotions.claimed_to_available_voluntary: Claimant releases without starting; clears claimed_by, appends note.
lifecycle_promotions.claimed_to_available_stale: Subsequent agent releases stale claim (>24h, no in_progress_at).

phase_a_manual: Write atoms to ATOMS.md §10 + ATOMS.json atoms[]
decomposition_workflow.phase_b_steward: Atomizer Steward at LEDGERS/BOXES/atoms/steward/ scans PROBs, proposes atoms to DRAFTS/ATOMIZATION/, sweeps stale claims, surfaces PROB-closure-eligible candidates.
decomposition_workflow.phase_c_runtime: Atoms become first-class Box Bus envelope citizens; atoms can subscribe to tier events; bus routes atom_completed events to dependent Boxes/Stewards.

atoms.verification: AUDIT COMPLETE — DECISION: **KEEP AS BEDROCK PRIMITIVE.** intelligence/ is well-formed bedrock and should NOT migrate to a Box, NOT archive, NOT consolidate. Tree shape: 7 top-level subareas (sales, leads, ops, cashflow, _runs, libraries, channels) × 2-6 child folders each = 24 leaf folders matching the 24 build_*.py scripts in 'Onboard Scripts/'. Total 119 files: 56 .md (canonical narrative outputs) + 30 .jsonl (structured data) + 24 .gitkeep (stubs for unintegrated scripts) + 8 .DS_Store (in .gitignore — won't be tracked) + 1 .json (build_conversation_intelligence output, paired with same-day .md per DEC-2026-04-30-001). Two subareas are operational today: sales/conversation/ (build_conversation_intelligence.py — surfaced by AnalyticsScreen ConversationIntelligencePanel) and sales/owner_stage/ (build_owner_stage_dashboards.py — surfaced by OwnerStagePanel via /api/analytics/owner_stage server endpoint, my earlier work via DEC-2026-04-30-001). MANIFEST.md (11924b) at root explicitly defines conventions: append-only dated files; source-of-truth inputs prefer people/*.json + venues/*.json; loader visibility N/A (UI surfaces aggregate views); ledger appends per-run. Per-script status table shows 24 scripts and their states (mostly 'blocked — needs raw Close export' for the legacy chain; 2 operational; rest pending). Subscribers identified: server.py (2 endpoints — /api/intelligence/conversation/latest and /api/analytics/owner_stage), mission_control_loader.js, screens.jsx. **Pairs cleanly with DEC-2026-04-30-001** synthesizer pattern (verified): the canonical server-synthesizes-JSON-from-markdown contract is exactly what intelligence/'s MANIFEST conventions describe. **Decision rationale: BEDROCK PRIMITIVE because** (a) it's data output not configuration/state — Boxes are for state-bearing entities; (b) ownership model is already clear via per-subarea-per-script mapping; (c) MANIFEST.md serves as a local orientation file (effectively a Box-lite); (d) the synthesizer pattern makes read pathways explicit and Box migration adds zero value; (e) it's actively used and forward-looking (21 of 24 scripts blocked but stubs exist in anticipation). **Optional follow-ups (NOT blocking this atom):** MANIFEST.md should cross-reference DEC-2026-04-30-001 in its header (currently predates that decision); MANIFEST.md status column should update as more build_*.py scripts get integrated (sales/conversation + sales/owner_stage are 'operational' now, MANIFEST may say 'blocked' for some). Atom acceptance criterion 'Decision recorded; verify intelligence/ pairs cleanly with the synthesizer pattern' satisfied.

atoms.acceptance_criteria: Atomizer Steward runnable at POST /api/agents/atomizer_steward/run. Smoke test: invoke against a PROB that lacks Atomized cross-link, verify it emits a proposed-atoms draft. Receipt.
atoms.area: LEDGERS/BOXES/atoms/steward + CCAgentindex/agents (or unified Box per DEC-004)
atoms.notes: The most-load-bearing interpreter. Closes the loop — agents propose atoms automatically, humans review.

atoms.title: Phase 5.5: Build second interpreter — Allowed-To-Know (closes PROB-001 fully)
atoms.description: Per Q4 second: Allowed-To-Know interpreter. Reads Client Box raw comms, classifies into the 4 buckets (comms_confirmed / internal_strategy / protected / approval_required) per SOURCE_OF_TRUTH §4. Output: filtered comms surface.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: Interpreter runnable. Smoke test: feed Hugo Casillas Box raw comms, verify 4-bucket classification matches SoT contract. Receipt. PROB-2026-04-28-001 status flips to closed.
atoms.notes: Closes the schema-design-criterion that's been partial since Phase 11. Real safety win.

atoms.title: Phase 5.6: Build third interpreter — Page-Asset impact
atoms.description: Per Q4 third: Page-Asset interpreter. Reads DECISIONS emits with scope:UI, classifies which page_asset_sitemap.md sections need updates, emits page-update candidates.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: Interpreter runnable. Smoke test: feed a fake UI-scoped DEC, verify it identifies the affected sitemap page section. Receipt.
atoms.notes: Closes the 'global decision → page sitemap update' loop. DEC-007 (sitemap Done Gate) becomes enforceable in Phase 6 runtime.

atoms.title: Phase 6.1: Add Box registry endpoint to server.py
atoms.description: GET /api/box_bus/registry — returns the full Box graph (boxes + tiers + subscribes/emits) as JSON. Reads from box.json files + authority_tiers + interpreters registry.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: Endpoint returns 200 with JSON shape matching scaffold §6. Smoke test via curl. Receipt.

atoms.title: Phase 6.2: Define envelope format (formal schema)
atoms.description: Per BOX_BUS_LEDGER §2.2: write the canonical envelope JSON schema. Validate against examples from temporal_continuity emits. Update Box Bus Ledger if schema evolves.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: Schema file at LEDGERS/BOXES/box_bus/registry/envelope.schema.json. Validator function in server.py. Existing emits validate clean.

atoms.title: Phase 6.3: Build subscription matcher
atoms.description: Function: given an emitted envelope, find every Box that subscribes to it (filter scope match + tier rules). Lives in server.py + reusable from steward agents.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: Matcher implementation. Unit test: 5 fake envelopes, verify match counts.

atoms.title: Phase 6.4: Build delivery queue
atoms.description: In-memory or file-backed queue of pending envelope deliveries. POST /api/box_bus/emit accepts envelopes, enqueues them, the matcher routes, the interpreter transforms, the destination receives.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: Queue implementation. POST /api/box_bus/emit returns 200 + envelope_id. Smoke test: emit one, verify it lands in destination's inbox (per Q5 resolution).

atoms.title: Phase 6.5: Receipt writer
atoms.description: Every delivery generates a receipt at LEDGERS/BOXES/<destination>/receipts/<YYYY-MM-DD>_<HHMM>_<envelope_id>.json. Records: source, destination, interpreter, transform diff, action taken.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: Receipt writer wired into delivery queue. Smoke test: emit one envelope, verify receipt lands at correct path with all fields populated.

atoms.title: Phase 6.6: Cycle detection + refusal
atoms.description: Per scaffold §10 Routing Done: cycle detection. If an envelope's route would revisit a Box already on the chain, refuse with a logged Open Problems entry.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: Cycle detector function. Unit test: feed a fake cycle, verify refusal + OPL entry. Smoke test on real graph: verify no false positives.

atoms.title: Phase 6.7: Dry-run mode + explain-route mode
atoms.description: Per scaffold §13.9: GET /api/box_bus/dry_run?source=<box>&event=<kind> simulates routing without delivering. Returns the full route + interpreter chain + would-be destinations. The 'why did this Box receive that state?' answer.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: Endpoint returns 200 with route trace JSON. UI affordance optional (Phase 7).

atoms.title: Phase 6.8: First end-to-end smoke test (one real cross-Box delivery)
atoms.description: Per scaffold §10 Runtime Done: send a real envelope from temporal_continuity emits to atoms (or another live destination). Verify it routes, the interpreter transforms, the receipt lands, the destination ledger updates. Phase 6 done when this is reproducible.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: Smoke test passes. Receipt at LEDGERS/BOXES/box_bus/receipts/2026-XX-XX_first_delivery.json. Phase Ledger §Phase Status updated. activity.jsonl phase_6_complete.
atoms.notes: Phase 6 close-out. Triggers Phase 7 readiness check.

atoms.title: Phase 7.1: Migrate Hugo Casillas Client Box to mature shape (per Q6 resolution)
atoms.description: Hugo's Client Box is the deepest existing client memory layer. Migration: ensure it has BOX.md (likely needs authoring), box.json (declares tier=domain, kind=client, owns=[01_comms.md, 01b_comms_verbatim.md, comms/, etc.]), local AGENTS.md (Hugo-specific guardrails per Brenda fee-waiver lesson DEC-005), receipts/.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: Auto/Client Boxes/Hugo Casillas/ has full mature Box shape. Existing client_ledger.md preserved as the LEDGER. Smoke test: client_box_steward (when authored) can read the Box.
atoms.notes: Per Q6 recommendation. Granularity ceiling — yellow flag, may decompose in practice.

atoms.title: Phase 7.2: Migrate one Page Box to mature shape
atoms.description: Pick a high-traffic page (Boxes or Intake per existing Page Ledgers). Author LEDGERS/BOXES/page_<route>/ following Phase 2 template. Box owns the Page Ledger md/json + sitemap entry by reference.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: LEDGERS/BOXES/page_<route>/ exists. Subscribes to UI-scoped DECISIONS via Page-Asset Interpreter (ATOM-0085). Receipt.
atoms.notes: Per scaffold §13.7. The first Page Box that uses the runtime.

atoms.title: Phase 7.3: Migrate Automation Box to mature shape
atoms.description: Automation lives across Auto/orchestrator/wiring/ and the automation UI page. Author LEDGERS/BOXES/automation/ governing both. box.json declares the cross-cutting nature (kind=automation, owns=multiple paths).
atoms.acceptance_criteria: LEDGERS/BOXES/automation/ exists with mature structure. Subscribes to scheduled-fire events. Receipt.

atoms.title: Phase 7 Meta: Decompose remaining Phase 7 migrations when prerequisites land
atoms.description: 27 remaining Client Boxes + 10 Staff Boxes + remaining Page Boxes + leaf Boxes (per scaffold §3 Tier 3). Atomized later — too many to author concrete atoms now without the migration template proven.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: Phase 7 starter atoms (0094-0096) verify the migration template. THEN this meta-atom is claimed to author the next batch (Atomizer Steward proposes; humans approve).
atoms.notes: Meta-atom. Don't attempt to atomize 40+ migration atoms before Phase 7.1-7.3 land.

atoms.title: Phase 8.1: Drift monitor — detects ledger MD/JSON desynchronization
atoms.description: Steward mode that walks every ledger MD + JSON pair, diffs structured fields, flags drift. Runs as part of weekly Box hygiene pass.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: Drift monitor implementation. Smoke test: artificially desync one mirror, verify detection. Output: per-ledger drift report. Receipt.

atoms.title: Phase 8.2: Stale-claim sweeper (per ATOMS.json claim protocol)
atoms.description: Per ATOMS.md §3.2 stale-claim rule. Atomizer Steward mode: scans atoms with claimed > 24h and no in_progress_at, releases them with notes appended. Already specced declaratively; this implements.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: Sweeper implementation. Smoke test: artificially stale one claim, verify auto-release. activity.jsonl line per release. Receipt.
atoms.notes: Implements the existing protocol — the Atomizer Steward's most-mechanical mode.

atoms.title: Phase 8.3: Source-of-Truth conflict audit
atoms.description: Steward mode that scans every Box's source_of_truth declaration vs SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md per-domain orderings. Flags conflicts.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: SoT audit implementation. Smoke test: introduce an artificial conflict, verify detection. Output: conflict report. Receipt. Phase Ledger §Phase Status updated.

atoms.title: Phase 8.4: Box shape validator
atoms.description: Steward mode that runs ATOM-0064's matrix against every Box. Flags missing required files. Suggests atoms to fix gaps.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: Validator implementation. Output: per-Box completeness percentage + missing-files list. Receipt. Optional: emits proposed atoms via Atomizer Steward integration.

atoms.title: Phase 8.5: Verify Phase 8 + Architecture Done declaration
atoms.description: Confirm ATOMs 0098-0101 shipped. Verify scaffold §10 Definition of Done For The Architecture is satisfied across all 6 categories. If not, surface gaps as new atoms. If yes, author DEC-2026-XX-XXX 'Box Network Architecture Complete' and update Phase Ledger to reflect Phase B fully complete (or whatever phase boundary lines up).
atoms.acceptance_criteria: Verification receipt. DEC entry IF criteria all met. Phase Ledger transition. PROB-2026-04-30-015 closure-eligible (surfaced for review, not auto-closed).
atoms.notes: The terminal atom for this PROB. Once all phases close, PROB-015 closes (or the architecture rolls into a new PROB if scope expands).

atoms.title: Backfill stale continuity ledgers + lock per-atom-completion update protocol in CLAUDE.md
atoms.description: Two-part atom surfacing a real protocol violation Jake flagged 2026-05-01: the Prime Directive in CLAUDE.md (and Definition of Done §11 Ledger Update Matrix) requires per-atom-completion updates to TCL §3 / GLOBAL §12 / COMMUNICATIONS / INDEX as appropriate, but the practical pattern this session has been edit-the-target-ledger-only. Result: TCL last touched 7h ago, COMM 3h ago, GLOBAL 7h ago, despite ~36 atoms shipped today. Part 1: update CLAUDE.md (project) with a mechanical per-atom-completion update checklist that triggers reflexively. Part 2: backfill TCL §3 + COMM (one handoff entry) + GLOBAL §12 with today's session summary to prove the rule works.
atoms.acceptance_criteria: (a) CLAUDE.md (project) gains a 'Per-Atom-Completion Update Protocol' section under or extending the existing Prime Directive — explicit checklist of WHICH ledgers must be updated per substantive atom completion, with examples of what counts as 'substantive'. (b) TCL §3 gains a 2026-04-30/2026-05-01 session summary entry capturing today's work (~36 atoms shipped + Box Network Architecture lock + Box Graph UI shipped). (c) COMMUNICATIONS_LEDGER gains COMM-2026-05-01-001 handoff explaining the protocol fix to next agent. (d) GLOBAL_LEDGER §12 gains a 2026-05-01 entry summarizing the day. (e) activity.jsonl gains entries for each backfill.
atoms.verification: CLAUDE.md (project) gained Per-Atom-Completion Update Protocol section after the existing Prime Directive — mechanical checklist (12 update destinations + cadence rule + substantive-vs-non-substantive criteria + 4 anti-patterns + orchestrator verification path). Backfill landed across 8 stale ledgers: TCL §3 (full session catch-up entry, ~70 lines) + TCL header bumped; GLOBAL §12 (full session catch-up entry, ~50 lines) + header bumped; COMMUNICATIONS COMM-2026-05-01-001 (highest-priority entry documenting the violation pattern, lessons, suggested actions, expiry conditions) + header bumped; PHASE header bumped with Phase B status; NORTH_STAR header bumped acknowledging architectural lock operationalizes existing NS goals (no new goals); DEPRECATION header bumped surfacing 4 abandoned-atom orphans + global_ledger legacy-path candidate; ASSET_WIDGET_MAP header bumped acknowledging Box Graph route + ChatRail components need formal §X registration; FILE_CONTENTS + FILE_DIRECTORY headers bumped acknowledging new files/dirs need full inventory refresh as follow-on atoms; INDEX header bumped to current state. SETTINGS verified as not-stale-by-omission. Activity ledger gains 12+ entries for the backfill chain.
atoms.do_not_undo_casually: Per-atom-completion update protocol is the FIX for the very thing we noticed — agents authoring rules they don't follow. Don't loosen the protocol or remove the checklist; tighten it if anything.
atoms.notes: Completed 2026-05-01T03:00Z. ~60 min real-time. Acknowledged the systemic protocol violation Jake surfaced. Continuity ledgers no longer 5-9h stale. CLAUDE.md mechanical checklist should prevent recurrence.

anti_patterns: Atoms that don't trace to a PROB (orphans violate the rule)
4-hour atoms (yellow flag — re-decompose if at all unsure)
Vague acceptance criteria (Improve X is not an acceptance criterion)
Re-claiming abandoned atoms as-is (abandonment is signal — reshape work)
Multiple agents working the same atom (single-writer rule)
Atomization without reading the PROB (produces shallow atoms missing close-criteria)
